Established in 2016, Max Estates aspires to build sustainable, grade A+ residential and commercial developments in Delhi NCR, with a focus on well-being. Our endeavour is to consciously create a unique confluence of spaces that enables collaboration, innovation, and community, and promotes holistic well-being of our consumers. Our current and planned developments are diversified across various asset classes, key strategic locations across Delhi NCR, as well as across the risk spectrum between delivered, nearing completion and under design projects.

Max Estates Limited is steadfastly emerging as a top brand in real estate at Delhi NCR. Max Estates Ltd. continues to be driven by its desire to enhance wellbeing through its exceptional experiences. Post Covid, the CRE industry witnessed a trend of flight to quality, with top-notch companies upgrading to developer owned Grade A+ spaces. Organisations have chosen well-maintained contemporary office complexes that offer better employee wellbeing, a sustainable outlook, and the best of amenities, which are suited to the future of work in the new normal. Aligning with the market trend and the need of the hour, we have built offices with a host of amenities and allocated dedicated areas for flexible workspace solutions across our assets as well. This has allowed us to be the preferred choice for tenants that are looking to occupy Grade A+ commercial office spaces in Delhi NCR. Our developments are now synonymous with quality and have enjoyed a surge in leasing demand from a reputed and diverse clientele, as well as commanded premium rentals, across micro markets. Our occupants belong to a varied set of industries including professional services, media, financial services, IT/ITES, real estate related businesses, manufacturing, etc.

On the residential front, our LiveWell philosophy guides the design and operational intent of our developments, with carefully selected locations, multitude of amenities, inclusive design, and a focus on sustainability becoming signature markers of all our developments. Operating in the premium and luxury segments, our residential developments are created to cater to discerning homeowners, who are looking to opt for a lifestyle of privacy and holistic well-being, across physical, social, emotional, and environmental aspects.
Max Estates Noida Private Limited (formerly Astiki Realty Private Limited)

Dear Stakeholders,

At Max Estates, our mission to "enhance the quality of life through the spaces we create" is more than just rhetorical; it is the driving force behind everything we do. As a leading real estate player in Delhi NCR, we recognize the pivotal role we play in shaping sustainable urban environments and positively impacting the lives of our stakeholders. 

From the outset, sustainability has been woven into the fabric of our organization. We understand the urgency of addressing climate change and environmental degradation, which is why we've embarked on a steadfast journey towards sustainability. Our actions are aligned with the global Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), as we endeavor to set a new standard for responsible development in the real estate sector. 

Our approach to sustainability is comprehensive and dynamic. We've implemented robust measures to boost energy efficiency and optimize resource utilization across all our projects. All our commercial office developments which are already delivered have secured LEED/IGBC Platinum Ratings for Green Building Strategies and WELL Health & Safety Rated. Our upcoming Residential developments including Estate 128 in Noida, and Estate 360 in Gurugram are designed for and pre-certified with IGBC Platinum rating. In addition, for Max Towers we have achieved LEED Operations & Maintenance Platinum certified. Moreover, our upcoming commercial developments including Max 65 in Gurgaon is pre-certified LEED Platinum for green building strategies, and Max Square II is pre-certified with IGBC Platinum rating exemplifying our commitment to green building practices. 

At the heart of our sustainability strategy lies the seamless integration of nature into our built environment. We prioritize green spaces and innovative landscape designs to foster well-being and combat the adverse effects of urbanization. Estate 128, with its sprawling green cover comprising over 70%, of the development, epitomizes our pledge to create thriving and healthy communities. 

Energy, water, waste and biodiversity are critical aspects for Max Estates to address because they significantly influence the environmental footprint of the built environment. Neglecting these areas can exacerbate resource depletion, climate change, pollution, and ecological imbalance, while prioritizing them can contribute to environmental restoration and long-term sustainable development.

At Max Estates, we have aligned our processes towards responsible use and protection of the natural environment through sustainable practices and conservation efforts, aiming to preserve the health and resilience of ecosystems for present and future generations. We have implemented Environment Management System (ISO 14001:2015) in our operations to reduce environmental impact and achieve our environmental goals.

We believe that building designs that prioritize waste management can facilitate reduction, sorting, and recycling of waste, minimizing the environmental footprint are crucial to streamline operations. Spaces dedicated to recycling and organic composting encourage segregation of waste effectively. Combined with diversion of waste from landfill, we champion a sustainable lifestyle for our residents and occupants.  

Water conservation is another cornerstone of our sustainability agenda. Recognizing the water challenges faced by urban centers, like Delhi NCR, we've embraced innovative solutions such as recycling water from onsite sewage treatment plants and implementing on-site treatment measures to curb groundwater depletion during construction activities. Innovative building designs can significantly reduce water consumption through the incorporation of efficient fixtures and water reuse systems. At our commercial development, Max Square, we have achieved a zero-waste water discharge facility through our water conservation interventions. 

In our unwavering commitment to transparency and accountability, we've published a Sustainability Note & our BRSR report, offering insights into our progress and future aspirations. Moreover, we have adopted global standards, such as GRESB, to benchmark our performance and drive continuous improvement in our environmental, social, and governance (ESG) practices. We have also begun our journey of computing and monitoring our GHG emissions to ultimately set targets, following the SBTi curriculum, and move towards net zero buildings. 

Since 2021, we have been on a structured ESG journey, delineating short-term, medium-term, and long-term goals to drive sustainable development. Having successfully achieved our medium-term objectives, we have undertaken a comprehensive review to refine and prioritize our ESG goals for FY26. These goals include: 

•	Strategize and develop targets for Net Zero emissions
•	Committing to SBTi for our Net Zero goal 
•	Life Cycle Embodied Carbon Analysis for Max Towers 
•	Explore partnerships to create a circular economy
•	Source Renewable energy for our delivered commercial assets  
•	Increase gender diversity at Max Estates Head Office level to 25%
•	Increase participation of women as labour on construction site
•	Academic tie-ups to increase awareness and interest of women in real estate
•	Provide dedicated channels for lodging grievances, thereby improving governance and efficient  
              stakeholder management

These specific and measurable goals underscore our dedication to advancing sustainability across all facets of our operations, from environmental stewardship to social responsibility and governance. By aligning our actions with these targets, we aim to strengthen our position as a leader in sustainable real estate development and create lasting value for our stakeholders.
Mr. Sahil Vachani, Vice Chairman and Managing Director (DIN:00761695)

Max Estates distinguishes itself in the real estate sector by strongly emphasising ethical conduct. This is achieved by implementing a comprehensive governance framework designed to combat corruption.

The company has a long-standing commitment to ethical business practices, which is reflected in the high standards of conduct expected from all employees. A cornerstone of this framework is a robust Whistleblower Policy that encourages the reporting of misconduct and ensures appropriate corrective actions are taken when necessary.

The following publicly available anti-corruption policies underscore Max Estates’ dedication to transparency and ethical operations:

•	Code of Conduct for Directors and Senior Management: Defines the ethical expectations for the company's leadership.
•	Whistleblower Policy: Encourages the reporting of potential wrongdoing while safeguarding whistleblowers.
•	Code of Conduct for Insider Trading: Establishes procedures to regulate, monitor, and report insider trading activities.
•	Code of Conduct for Handling incoming business courtesies: Provided guidelines to ensure no unfair business practices and quid-pro-quo arrangements exist or are pursued between employees and external stakeholders.
•	Related Party Transactions Policy: Provides guidelines for fair and transparent interactions with affiliated entities.

Building a Culture of Integrity
These policies ensure that all employees, regardless of their role, understand the importance of ethical conduct. They are embedded into the onboarding process for new hires and are reinforced regularly through emails, training workshops, and ongoing communication.
By placing a strong focus on ethical practices and transparency, Max Estates reinforces its reputation as a leader in responsible business conduct within the real estate industry.
Weblink to our relevant Policies and Code of Conduct:  https://maxestates.in/investors

Essential Indicators

Q2 a - Max Estates is committed to sustainable sourcing, in line with the company’s long-standing vision and corporate policies for sustainable development within the real estate sector. Our sourcing practices comply with applicable environmental regulations and include ongoing evaluation of low-carbon alternatives for both structural and non-structural materials. We also actively explore advancements in modular construction technologies and place a strong emphasis on reducing and recycling construction and demolition waste.

Additionally, we maintain stringent standards to ensure that all suppliers, manufacturers, and contractors comply with relevant health, safety, and environmental legislation. Max Estates maintains a zero-tolerance policy toward forced, bonded, or child labour across its supply chain. We also promote collaborative partnerships with our suppliers to drive continuous product innovation and minimize environmental impact.

Weblink to our https://maxestates.in/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/MEL_ESG_Sustainable-Procurement_Public.pdf

Q2 b - Max Estates mandates that all its developments achieve at least LEED/IGBC Gold certification. In line with this overarching commitment, we adhere to the guidelines set forth by LEED/IGBC for material usage in sustainable buildings. For instance, only low-VOC paints are utilised in our assets, in compliance with LEED/IGBC standards. Additionally, we encourage the use of the materials with GreenPro Ecolabel, which are products that qualify as Green Products.

Q4 - Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is currently not applicable to the core activities of our organization, as we are primarily engaged in real estate development and do not manufacture or sell products that fall under EPR mandates (such as plastic packaging, electrical and electronic equipment, or batteries).

However, we have instituted a structured system for the segregation and responsible collection of reusable and recyclable materials at all our project sites. These materials are managed through authorized agencies who are licensed to handle and dispose of or recycle such waste streams in an environmentally compliant manner. This approach ensures that even in the absence of formal EPR obligations, our waste management practices align with the principles of resource recovery, circularity, and environmental responsibility.

We continuously monitor regulatory developments and are prepared to adapt our waste collection processes should EPR applicability change in the future.


Leadership Indicators

Q1 - While an LCA was not conducted this year, we are taking proactive steps towards sustainability. We plan to conduct an LCA of our buildings to assess their environmental impact, including emissions and embodied carbon.

Q2 - We are committed to conducting a full LCA in the near future to gain a deeper understanding of the environmental and social impacts associated with our operations.

Q3 - Data collection for the percentage of recycled or reused input material used in our operations is currently under development. We recognise the importance of resource efficiency and are actively working to implement a system to track and report this metric in future BRSR reports.

Q4 - Max Estates' core business involves real estate development and management, not the production or sale of consumer products with a typical end-of-life cycle ("end-of-life" for us might refer to demolition or major renovations in our constructed and operated buildings). Therefore currently, the question is not directly applicable.

Yes. Max Estates have implemented a Safety Management System, including a comprehensive Safety Manual
and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs). Our Health and Safety manual for projects applies to all individuals working at the project sites. Additionally, each project site may present unique challenges, and site-specific safety plans are developed and implemented by the vendor/contractor to address these concerns. A site safety management committee is responsible for monitoring the execution of each safety plan. Regular internal and external safety audits are conducted to ensure compliance with safety guidelines. Any safety gaps, incidents, or operational improvements are communicated to the site safety committee and the corporate safety team. Lessons learned from each site are recorded at the corporate level and incorporated into the safety instruction manual, which is prominently displayed at the site through various signage in both Hindi and English. We have successfully obtained Occupational Health and Safety certification (ISO 45001), demonstrating our commitment to maintaining the highest safety standards across all operations.

Max Estates appoints a dedicated safety manager for each site under development and operational assets. The respective site safety managers are responsible for training, following the SOP, and inspecting and maintaining the safety logs.

Max Estates conducts weekly site safety inspections, generating reports based on these inspections. Copies
of the completed inspection reports are kept on-site and are available for review at all times. Any deficiencies
identified or unsafe practices discovered during these inspections are promptly addressed through remedial
actions. Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA) is conducted as per the Safety SOP on both routine and non-routine basis.

The entire portfolio adheres to the National Building Code (NBC) 2016, ensuring the safety of both premises
and employees.

We have successfully received ISO 9001 certification for Quality Management, ISO 45001 certification for Occupational Health and Safety, and ISO 14001 certification for Environmental Management.

In alignment with our “WorkWell philosophy”, all of Max Estates’ assets are people-centric, with all possible measures having been incorporated for enhancing workplace health and well-being not only at the design stage but also whilst operating the assets. These measures address various aspects such as indoor air quality, water quality, daylight access, biophilia, hygiene parameters, healthy food choices, lifestyle etc. We ensure safety through structured daily toolbox talks, periodic safety audits, mandatory use of personal protective equipment (PPE), comprehensive emergency response plans, regular safety trainings, and strict adherence to applicable statutory norms.

This collectively helps in promoting physical, psychological, and social well-being among all building occupants. To implement these measures, design, operational and policy level interventions are required which are well incorporated in all the assets.

Max Estates Limited had conducted a comprehensive assessment in the past to identify its critical stakeholders, categorising them into primary and secondary groups based on priority. Primary stakeholders include investors, employees, tenants, suppliers, and contractors, while secondary stakeholders encompass local and regional communities, government entities, and media outlets. Both primary and secondary stakeholders play vital roles in the company’s growth, providing financial and strategic support.

This stakeholder mapping exercise provided Max Estates Limited with valuable insights into the key stakeholders and their expectations from the business. The selection criteria for primary and secondary stakeholders were based on their direct or indirect dependence on the company’s activities, real estate portfolio, services, performance, or influence on strategic and operational decisions.

To enhance organisational learning and improve decision-making, a cross-functional team comprising experts
from finance, law, operations, and management was formed. This team conducted a materiality assessment to gather deeper insights, helping Max Estates Limited make more informed decisions.

Additionally, we maintain consistent engagement with our stakeholders through various communication
channels. Acknowledging that each stakeholder group has distinct expectations, we customise our engagement efforts to address the topics that are most relevant to each group.

Max Estates prioritizes stakeholder engagement on economic, environmental, and social (EES) topics. We utilize a multi-channel approach to facilitate consultation on economic, environmental, and social (EES) topics:

•	Formal Channels: Annual surveys, meetings with key stakeholder groups (investors, employees, communities).

•	Informal Channels: 
o	Employees Survey: Feedback mechanisms like employee surveys.

o	Social media, Articles & Blogs: We actively engage with stakeholders on social media platforms, share informative articles and blogs, and encourage online discussions on relevant EES issues.

o	Website and Marketing Collaterals: Our website and marketing materials are designed to transparently communicate our EES commitments and encourage stakeholder engagement.

All feedback received through these channels is meticulously analyzed and summarized. This comprehensive overview is then presented to the Board of Directors, with our GM (Engineering) providing updates on all ESG matters. This ensures that stakeholder voices are not only heard but also directly inform and influence our strategic decision-making across all economic, environmental, and social considerations.

Max Estates actively engages in stakeholder consultation to support the identification and management of environmental and social issues. Here’s how:

•	Validating Material Topics: Our initial materiality assessment conducted in the past, built on direct stakeholder consultation, established core EES priorities like environmental impact and employee well-being. This historical input continues to inform and validate the priority topics identified in our current internal materiality review, such as Climate Change, Energy Management, Employee Development and Engagement, and Customer Health and Safety.

•	Driving Policy and Action:
•	Climate & Energy: Investor and community concerns about environmental impact directly led to our GHG emission reduction targets and investments in energy-efficient designs. Our GHG emissions and energy savings KPIs reflect this.

•	Employee Well-being: Consistent employee feedback on growth and development has driven our robust training programs and mentorship initiatives, tracked by training hours.

•	Customer Safety: Customer feedback on health and safety influences our stringent safety protocols and our focus on CSAT scores for safety aspects.

•	Community Impact: Community input guides our policies for local employment and our engagement in community development initiatives.

In essence, stakeholder feedback is integral to Max Estates. It doesn't just inform our EES priorities; it directly translates into tangible policies and activities, ensuring we remain responsive and responsible to all our stakeholders.

Yes, Max Estates recognizes the importance of supporting vulnerable and marginalized stakeholder groups. We actively engage with these communities and take concrete actions to address their concerns:

•	Community Development Programs: We go beyond just neighbouring communities. We partner with local NGOs and organizations like Habitat for Humanity (HFH) to support initiatives that address the needs of underprivileged communities across our project locations, promoting inclusive development.

•	Human Rights and Labour Welfare Policy: We have a comprehensive Labour Welfare Policy that ensures fair treatment and working conditions for all our employees and those employed by our contractors. This policy adheres to all applicable labour laws and goes beyond legal requirements to promote worker well-being.

•	Supplier Code of Conduct: Our Contractor Code of Conduct emphasizes fair labour practices throughout our supply chain. We work with suppliers who share our commitment to protecting vulnerable workers and ensuring ethical business practices.

These examples demonstrate Max Estates' dedication to creating a positive social impact and fostering inclusive growth. We continuously explore new avenues for engagement and remain committed to strengthening our social responsibility efforts.

Max Estates Limited has onboarded Smart Joules as a vendor to track electricity consumption across its assets in a real-time basis. This system helps Max Estates monitor consumption patterns and develop effective strategies for reducing energy use. In addition, Max Estates is actively exploring options for renewable power purchases to decrease reliance on grid electricity for its commercial assets. To further support sustainable energy use, rooftop solar generation is being considered for assets that currently lack solar generation capabilities.

In its efforts to reduce GHG emissions across under-construction projects, Max Estates is implementing several energy-efficient initiatives, including:
» Double glazing unit glasses fixed in all commercial building façades across assets to reduce air conditioning load.
» The Heat Recovery Mechanism (DOAS) recycles exhaust air to retract temperature, enhancing energy efficiency.
» Utilising AAC (Autoclaved Aerated Concrete) blocks in construction to improve thermal insulation and enhance
energy efficiency.
» The implementation of cooling as a service module to optimise equipment efficiency and reduce
electricity consumption.
» Exploring solar power purchases to help reduce Scope 2 emissions and facilitate a transition to green energy.
» Installing sensor-based LED lights in common areas to further reduce electricity consumption.

These combined efforts reflect Max Estates’ commitment to enhancing sustainability and reducing environmental
impact across its developments.

Max Estates Limited has created a comprehensive waste management strategy that includes identifying different types of waste, such as hazardous and non-hazardous waste, and ensuring appropriate waste management mechanisms are in place. Further, we have elaborated waste management on-site, segregating waste in accordance with various regulations. Specifically, we segregate waste in the garbage room under the Solid Waste Management Rules, 2016; plastic waste under the Plastic Waste Management Rules, 2016; Construction and Demolition Waste under the Construction and Demolition Waste Management Rules, 2016; E-waste under the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2016; used lube oil from DG sets and machines under the Hazardous Wastes (Management and Transboundary Movement) Rules, 2016; and batteries waste under the Batteries (Management and Handling) Rules, 2001. This includes minimizing waste at the source, promoting recycling and diversion of waste from landfills, in-office development, applying appropriate methods to handle and dispose of electronic waste, ensuring waste storage, working with tenants to ensure proper waste management, securing waste and debris disposal, and deploying construction and demolition waste handling, storage & diversion techniques.

For properties in Noida 100% waste diversion from landfill is currently being followed through help of onboarded vendors. Furthermore, cigarette waste recycling initiative has been started across properties to ensure the cigarette waste is being recycled into products that can be bought from onboarded vendors.

Q5 - Yes, Max Estates Limited has a comprehensive Cyber Security Policy, effective since February 12, 2023, to manage cybersecurity and data privacy risks.
This policy mandates strict standards for protecting the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of all IT assets and information.

Our Cybersecurity & Data Privacy Framework:

Guided by the NIST Cybersecurity Framework v1.1 (2018), our approach covers five key areas:

1.	Identify: Proactive management of IT assets, understanding our business environment, ensuring regulatory compliance (including privacy laws), escalating risks to the Executive Risk Committee, conducting Security Impact Assessments, and managing supply chain risks.

2.	Protect: Implementing robust measures including strong Identity Management and Access Control (multi-factor authentication, least privilege), mandatory Awareness & Training for all staff, comprehensive Data Security (encryption, secure destruction, lifecycle management), well-defined Information Protection Processes (backups, vulnerability management), secure Maintenance protocols, and advanced Protective Technologies (network protection, logging).	

3.	Detect: Continuous monitoring and detection processes for security events, including identifying anomalies, continuous vulnerability scans, and well-defined detection procedures.

4.	Respond: Clear incident response plans within our Business Continuity & Resilience Framework, ensuring trained personnel, consistent reporting, and effective communication and mitigation strategies.

5.	Recover: Robust recovery plans, regularly rehearsed, with a focus on lessons learned and effective public relations management after an event.

This integrated framework underscores our commitment to data protection and robust cybersecurity.
